mission.title: Install and Configure Radius on Kubernetes
mission.description: Radius is a cloud-native application platform that simplifies app development and management for teams building cloud-native applications. Radius is installed using the rad CLI, which deploys the Radius control plane into the radius-system namespace on your Kubernetes cluster.

steps.title: Install the rad CLI
steps.description:
Install the Radius CLI on your workstation.

**macOS/Linux:**
```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/radius-project/radius/main/deploy/install.sh" | /bin/bash
```

**macOS (via wget):**
```bash
wget -q "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/radius-project/radius/main/deploy/install.sh" -O - | /bin/bash
```

Verify the installation:
```bash
rad version
```

steps.title: Initialize Radius
steps.description:
Initialize Radius on your Kubernetes cluster. This deploys the Radius control plane into the radius-system namespace:

```bash
rad init
```

This interactive command will:
- Install the Radius control plane in the radius-system namespace
- Create a default environment
- Optionally configure cloud providers (Azure, AWS)

For non-interactive setup with all options:
```bash
rad init --full
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Radius control plane pods are running:

```bash
kubectl get pods -n radius-system
```

You should see pods for the Radius controller and other components in Running state.

Also verify with the rad CLI:
```bash
rad env list
```

steps.title: Install Radius to Kubernetes directly (alternative)
steps.description:
You can also install Radius directly to Kubernetes without the interactive init:

```bash
rad install kubernetes
```

This installs the Radius control plane matching the rad CLI version. Then create an environment:

```bash
rad env create my-env
rad env switch my-env
```

uninstall.title: Uninstall Radius from Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
Remove the Radius control plane from your cluster:

```bash
rad uninstall kubernetes
```

This removes all Radius components from the radius-system namespace.

uninstall.title: Clean up namespace
uninstall.description:
If the namespace remains, delete it manually:

```bash
kubectl delete namespace radius-system
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Radius
upgrade.description:
First upgrade the rad CLI to the latest version, then upgrade the cluster installation:

```bash
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/radius-project/radius/main/deploy/install.sh" | /bin/bash
rad install kubernetes --reinstall
```

Verify:
```bash
rad version
kubectl get pods -n radius-system
```

troubleshooting.title: rad init fails
troubleshooting.description:
If rad init fails, check that kubectl is configured and can reach your cluster:

```bash
kubectl cluster-info
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Radius pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the Radius controller pod logs:

```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=radius -n radius-system
kubectl describe pods -n radius-system
```

troubleshooting.title: rad CLI not found
troubleshooting.description:
If the rad command is not found after installation, ensure it is in your PATH. You can set a custom install directory:

```bash
export RADIUS_INSTALL_DIR=/path/to/custom/dir
curl -fsSL "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/radius-project/radius/main/deploy/install.sh" | /bin/bash
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Radius will have the control plane pods running in the radius-system namespace, the rad CLI available in your PATH, and at least one environment configured. You can then define and deploy Radius applications using Bicep templates.

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster (v1.24+), kubectl configured to access the cluster, and curl to download the rad CLI.
